name: nonblocking-loop
description: Replace blocking time.sleep() with ticks_ms()-based scheduling. Essential for responsive devices that need to handle WiFi, sensors, and buttons simultaneously.
problem: time.sleep() blocks everything — no WiFi check, no button response, no sensor read. Beginners write linear code that freezes the device.
solution: Use time.ticks_ms() + time.ticks_diff() to create multiple independent timers running in parallel within a single loop.
key_insights: time.ticks_diff(a, b) handles the 32-bit wrap (~298 hours) correctly. NEVER use a - b directly.
Put the smallest possible sleep_ms() at the bottom of loop (10ms is good) — feeds WDT without wasting CPU.
CPU at 10ms loop interval = ~0.1% utilization. You can have dozens of scheduled tasks with zero performance impact.
Callback functions should be FAST (<1ms). If a task takes longer, split it into state-machine steps.

variants.bare_ticks: No scheduler class — just if ticks_diff(now, last) > INTERVAL: for each task. Fewer objects, less memory.
variants.priority_tasks: Two Scheduler instances — high priority runs every loop, low priority only when idle.
variants.cooperative_multitask: Each task is a generator function. yield from main loop advances one step per call.

pitfalls: time.ticks_diff(now, next_run) >= 0 — not 'now >= next_run'. Direct comparison breaks at wrap-around!
Running heavy callbacks in tick() blocks other tasks. Keep callbacks lightweight.
time.sleep_ms(0) burns CPU at 100%. Always use at least 1ms sleep to yield to WiFi/OS tasks.
